name: Puppeteer
flavor: A marionettist who never means to enter the fight himself — a web of near-invisible wire turns foes into puppets and allies into partners, moved exactly where the show demands.

proficiencies.skills: Choose two from Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Performance, Persuasion, and Sleight of Hand

parryReskin: You are no blocker at all — a curtain of near-invisible wire snaps up between you and the blow at the last instant, trying to deflect what your own body never could. It is a last resort, not a specialty: your effective Parry DC runs the worst in the roster, so a clean Full Dodge is rare, and more often the wire only barely turns aside what it can't fully stop.
riposte: On a Full Dodge (your Parry d20 roll comes up strictly above your effective Parry DC), you may attach a String to the triggering attacker at no action cost and without spending your bonus action. This follows your normal String cap — if you are already at your cap, you must detach an existing String to make room. A small, thematic reward for a clean dodge, not a counterattack.

engine.name: Strings
engine.description: Lengths of near-invisible wire you fix to creatures around you. A String is not spent like currency — it is an attachment that stays in place, keying your control features to whatever it's tied to, until you choose to let go of it or something forces you to.
engine.generation: Attach a String to a creature you can see within 60 feet as a bonus action, or automatically at no action cost when you hit a creature with your Control Wires. You may never have more Strings attached at once than your String cap; attaching a new one beyond your cap requires detaching an existing String first (no action).
engine.spend: Strings aren't consumed to use your control features — they are the prerequisite for them. Manipulate, Tangle, Disrupting Pull, Guiding Pull, and Grand Marionette all require a String already attached to the target; using them doesn't remove the String. A String stays attached until you detach it (no action), the creature moves beyond your 60-foot leash range, or you become incapacitated, in which case all of your Strings snap at once.
engine.cap: proficiency bonus (2 at levels 1-4, 3 at level 5; Web of Strings adds 1 more starting at 5th level)

features.name: Strings
features.description: You gain the Strings engine. As a bonus action, you attach a String to a creature you can see within 60 feet (90 at 5th level) — or attach one for free when you hit a creature with your Control Wires. You can maintain up to your String cap (your proficiency bonus) at once. Every other control feature you have keys off a creature you already have a String on; see your Strings engine for how Strings are maintained and lost.

features.name: Strings of Will
features.description: Your art runs on focus and anticipation, not muscle or bluster: you use your Wisdom modifier for attack and damage rolls made with a finesse weapon you're proficient with (your Control Wires, Dagger, or Whip). Any saving throw a Puppeteer feature forces on a creature uses your control DC, equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Bodyguard Strings
features.description: You never meant to take a hit yourself. A number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), which you regain when you finish a long rest, when you are hit by an attack while you have a String attached to another creature within 10 feet of you, you can redirect the attack's damage onto that stringed creature instead of resolving it against you: it takes the full damage of the hit and you take none. Redirecting resolves the attack, so you cannot also Parry the same hit, and it costs you no action or reaction. The wire yanks it into the path — an unwilling creature gets no save; a willing ally may choose to refuse.

features.name: Manipulate
features.description: As an action, choose any number of creatures you currently have a String attached to, each within 60 feet of you. For each one, you pull or push it up to 15 feet in a direction of your choice; this forced movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks and can drag a creature over or around minor obstacles it could normally cross, but never through solid objects or off a fall it can't survive without consent. An unwilling creature resists: it makes a Charisma saving throw against your control DC, and on a success it isn't moved. A willing creature — typically an ally — is simply moved, no save required, letting you yank a friend out of danger or into position exactly as freely as you yank a foe into a wall. Manipulate deals no damage on its own.

features.name: Tangle
features.description: As an action, choose one creature you have a String attached to within 60 feet. If it is unwilling, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your control DC or become restrained as wire cinches tight around it; at the end of each of its turns it may repeat the save, ending the restrained condition on a success. If it is willing — an ally you're steadying rather than binding — you instead brace it with your strings, granting it advantage on the next Strength or Dexterity saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn. Measured control: this holds a target in place or steadies a friend, it doesn't end a fight by itself.

features.name: Disrupting Pull
features.description: When a creature you have a String attached to makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction to wrench its wire; it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your control DC or have disadvantage on that attack roll. Alternatively, when a creature you have a String attached to is about to take a reaction (an opportunity attack, for instance), you can use your reaction to yank its string instead; it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your control DC or that reaction fizzles, accomplishing nothing. Either use spends your one reaction for the round, the same reaction Parry and opportunity attacks draw from — you get one disruption, one Parry, or one opportunity attack per round, never a combination.

features.name: Guiding Pull
features.description: When a creature you have a String attached to and that is willing to be moved (typically an ally) is targeted by an attack you can see, you can use your reaction to yank it up to 10 feet toward you or to another space within your leash range, before the attack roll is made. If this pulls the target out of the attacker's reach (for a melee attack) or breaks the attacker's line of sight or range (for a ranged attack), the attack automatically misses; otherwise it resolves normally against the target's new position. This draws on the same reaction as Parry, Disrupting Pull, and opportunity attacks — you still get only one reaction per round.

features.name: Grand Marionette
features.description: As an action, choose one hostile creature you have a String attached to within 60 feet. It must make a Charisma saving throw against your control DC. On a failure, you seize its body for this turn: you may move it up to its speed (its movement can provoke opportunity attacks as normal) and force it to make a single weapon attack, using its own statistics and weapon, against a target of your choice within its reach or range. Resolve that attack normally, with the creature rolling its own attack and damage. This is your only 'puppet an action' effect at these levels — it grants exactly one forced attack, not a full turn's worth of actions, and the controlled creature otherwise loses the rest of its action and its bonus action for the turn (it keeps its reaction). You can use Grand Marionette once per turn, and only on a creature you already have a String on.

features.name: Web of Strings
features.description: Your control reaches farther and holds more of it at once: your leash range increases from 60 to 90 feet for every String-based feature you have, and your String cap increases by 1 in addition to whatever your proficiency bonus already grants it.

keyStats.label: Control DC
keyStats.note: Save DC for your String control effects (Manipulate, Tangle, Grand Marionette, etc.).

keyStats.label: Strings Cap
keyStats.note: Most active Strings you can maintain.

keyStats.label: Parry Base DC
keyStats.note: Worst parrier in the roster; he avoids melee. Lower is better.
